key_insight: A ReAct-style AI agent can query TCGA clinical data in natural language, run statistical analyses, and deliver auditable answers with high accuracy.
clinical_relevance: This could help researchers quickly access and compare oncology clinical variables, survival outcomes, and metadata that are otherwise difficult to retrieve from TCGA files.
method: LLM agent selects among 8 tools for data extraction, descriptive stats, survival analysis, hypothesis testing, and CDR verification.
Uses an iterative ReAct loop to reason over intermediate outputs and adjust its next step.
Benchmarked on 440 queries across 5 difficulty tiers with dual scoring for numerical accuracy and clinical completeness.
evidence_signal: The system reached 93.4% overall accuracy, including 100% for single-patient lookups and 99.1% for cohort statistics, and outperformed fixed, single-pass, and RAG baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on tool design and benchmark scope, and the system is only as reliable as the underlying clinical data and verification coverage.
paper_title: A ReAct Agentic AI System for Natural Language Querying and Statistical Analysis of The Cancer Genome Atlas Clinical Data
